2604.04448.title: PSY-STEP: Structuring Therapeutic Targets and Action Sequences for Proactive Counseling Dialogue Systems
2604.04448.paper_summary: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) aims to identify and restructure automatic negative thoughts pertaining to involuntary interpretations of events, yet existing counseling agents struggle to identify and address them in dialogue settings. To bridge this gap, we introduce STEP, a dataset that models CBT counseling by explicitly reflecting automatic thoughts alongside dynamic, action-level counseling sequences. Using this dataset, we train STEPPER, a counseling agent that proactively elicits automatic thoughts and executes cognitively grounded interventions. To further enhance both decision accuracy and empathic responsiveness, we refine STEPPER through preference learning based on simulated, synthesized counseling sessions. Extensive CBT-aligned evaluations show that STEPPER delivers more clinically grounded, coherent, and personalized counseling compared to other strong baseline models, and achieves higher counselor competence without inducing emotional disruption.
2604.04448.paper_summary_zh: 認知行為療法（CBT）旨在識別和重構與事件的非自願解釋相關的自動負面思維，然而現有的輔導代理在對話環境中難以識別和解決這些問題。為了填補這一空白，我們介紹了STEP，一個通過明確反映自動思維和動態行動層級輔導序列來建模CBT輔導的數據集。利用這個數據集，我們訓練了STEPPER，一個主動引發自動思維並執行基於認知的干預的輔導代理。為了進一步提高決策準確性和同理心反應，我們通過基於模擬的合成輔導會話的偏好學習來改進STEPPER。廣泛的CBT對齊評估顯示，STEPPER提供的輔導在臨床基礎、連貫性和個性化方面優於其他強基準模型，並在不引起情緒干擾的情況下實現了更高的輔導者能力。
2604.04448.author: Jihyun Lee et.al.
2604.04448.authors: Jihyun Lee, Yejin Min, Yejin Jeon, SungJun Yang, Hyounghun Kim, Gary Geunbae Lee
